content: We can reframe the performance difference between curiosity-driven RL methods (ICM, RND) and hand-tuned curious agents as a function of their implicit memory usage overhead: the intrinsic reward bonus of ICM can be made adaptive to the agent's available working memory budget, such that sparse memory environments automatically favor the simpler reward structure of dedicated curious agents over large, memory-heavy ICM implementations.
source_topics: curiosity driven reinforcement learning ICM RND compared to curious agent
Memory-efficient ICM implementation for large-scale autonomous agents
trigger_topic: curiosity driven reinforcement learning ICM RND compared to curious agent
